title: 5 Materials to Avoid in Intimate Products

hook_slide.headline: Are Your Intimacy Products Harming You?
hook_slide.subline: Discover 5 common materials to avoid for body-safe pleasure and lasting wellness.
hook_slide.image_concept: A soft, out-of-focus background of warm, dark textures (velvet, silk), with a hint of metallic sheen, creating an air of mystery and luxury.

education_slides.headline: Beyond the Surface
education_slides.body: Not all products are created equal. Understanding the materials in your intimate wellness tools is crucial for both your health and shared pleasure.
education_slides.image_concept: Close-up of a hand gently touching a smooth, ambiguous dark object, emphasizing texture and care.

education_slides.headline: The Bacteria Trap: Porous Plastics
education_slides.body: Avoid 'jelly,' PVC, and TPR. Their porous nature makes them impossible to fully sanitize, creating a breeding ground for harmful bacteria and infections.
education_slides.image_concept: Abstract, slightly blurred image of microscopic textures, hinting at unseen elements, in a muted, warm palette.

education_slides.headline: Fragile Fun: Untempered Glass
education_slides.body: While tempered glass is safe, cheap or untempered glass products pose a serious risk of breakage, leading to injury. Prioritize tempered, non-porous options.
education_slides.image_concept: A subtle, elegant silhouette of a glass object, with a hint of light refraction, against a dark, warm background.

education_slides.headline: Questionable Metals & Alloys
education_slides.body: Non-surgical grade or plated metals can corrode, leach heavy metals, and cause allergic reactions. Stick to body-safe stainless steel or medical-grade silicone.
education_slides.image_concept: A sleek, polished metallic surface reflecting a warm glow, suggesting quality and safety.

education_slides.headline: Natural Isn't Always Safe
education_slides.body: Beautiful as they are, porous stones and woods (like unsealed jade or untreated wood) cannot be sterilized. They harbor bacteria, risking infection and discomfort.
education_slides.image_concept: Textured, organic surface (e.g., polished dark wood grain or smooth stone) with soft, diffused lighting.

education_slides.headline: Hidden Chemicals: Phthalates & Parabens
education_slides.body: Often found in cheap lubricants and cleaners, these endocrine-disrupting chemicals can interfere with hormones and impact reproductive health. Read labels carefully.
education_slides.image_concept: Minimalist design with stylized chemical symbols or molecular structures, in a clean, sophisticated style.

education_slides.headline: Your Body Deserves Better
education_slides.body: Prioritizing body-safe materials protects your health, enhances pleasure, and builds trust in your intimate journey. Invest in quality for lasting wellness.
education_slides.image_concept: A sense of comfort and protection, perhaps soft fabrics gently draped, with warm, inviting light.

framework_slide.title: Body-Safe Intimacy Checklist
framework_slide.image_concept: Clean, organized infographic layout with subtle icons for each point, set against a calming, dark background.

cta_slide.headline: Curated by Dark Fantasy
cta_slide.image_concept: Dark Fantasy logo (minimalist, elegant) centered on a rich, warm, textured background (e.g., deep burgundy velvet or dark, polished wood).

caption: Elevate your intimate wellness by choosing wisely. Your body deserves the best, and understanding materials is key to safe, joyful exploration.
